paragraph: Ridgewood is almost entirely a single-family market, with 78 homes sold over the last six months at a median of $1,182,500. This is a real seller's market. Seventy-three percent of homes sold above asking, the sale-to-list ratio is sitting at 1.054, and the median time on market is 13 days. Sellers are dictating terms, and priced homes are moving quickly. The range is wide, from $620,000 to $4,325,000, which tells you the market absorbs everything from entry-level single-family buys to significant estates. Condos and townhouses barely register here with three sales at a median of $599,000, so if you're house hunting in Ridgewood, you're shopping single-family. The decisive question isn't market heat. It's whether you can find the home you want at a price you're willing to pay in this competitive environment where the best properties don't stay on the market long.
